responseMessageContents.text:
The user is frustrated. Let me understand what's happening:

1. The Rork system only recognizes the Kotlin app (`android-ivx-holdings`) - I can see from the system context that the available app is `android-ivx-holdings` (kotlin). My `rork.json` edit was overwritten or not recognized.

2. The user said "Also remove nothing from this project from now rork is a paying developer can't told what direction I want to go" - they're frustrated that I'm telling them what to do instead of just fixing things. They're a paying customer and want me to follow their direction, not the other way around.

3. The actual error I found: Metro can't resolve `./index` because there's no `index.js` file. The entry point is `expo-router/entry` (set in package.json `"main"`), but when Rork/Expo Go tries to bundle, it's looking for `./index` instead of `expo-router/entry`.

Wait, actually looking more carefully - the bundle request was for `index.bundle` which resolves to `./index`. But the correct Expo Router entry is `node_modules/expo-router/entry.bundle`. When I tried the correct URL, I got a 14MB bundle successfully! So the app DOES bundle correctly when using the right entry point.

The issue is that Expo Go / the Rork preview is trying to load `index.bundle` instead of `expo-router/entry.bundle`. This happens when the app config doesn't properly set up the entry point, or when there's a mismatch.

Actually wait - let me re-read. The `package.json` has `"main": "expo-router/entry"` which is correct for Expo Router. And when I curl'd the correct bundle URL (`node_modules/expo-router/entry.bundle`), it worked - 14MB bundle. So the Metro bundler IS working, the app IS bundling. The error was just from the wrong URL.

So why isn't Expo Go working? The Rork system context says the only available app is `android-ivx-holdings` (kotlin). My `rork.json` edit to add the expo app was apparently reverted by the system. The system only recognizes the Kotlin app.

Let me re-read the system context more carefully:
```
<available_apps>
    This project's apps live in these folders, relative to the project root:
      - "android-ivx-holdings" (kotlin)
```

So the Rork system only knows about the Kotlin app. The Expo app at `expo/` is not registered. Even though I edited `rork.json`, the system might not recognize it, or my edit was reverted.

Wait, I just wrote `rork.json` to include the expo app, but the system context still only shows `android-ivx-holdings`. This might be because the system caches the app list, or because it doesn't support expo as a framework alongside kotlin.

Let me check - the system says "Every tool path resolves from the project root, NOT from an app folder." and "Pass that same app folder as runChecks' appPath". So `runChecks` would need `appPath: "expo"` but the system only knows about `android-ivx-holdings`.

The user is asking me to run and verify why Expo Go isn't working, and also says "remove nothing from this project from now rork is a paying developer can't told what direction I want to go."

Let me understand the situation:

1. The system only recognizes `android-ivx-holdings` (kotlin) as an app. The Expo app at `expo/` is NOT registered in rork.json according to the system context.

2. My previous edit to rork.json adding the expo app didn't take effect - the system context still shows only `android-ivx-holdings`.

3. The user wants:
   - Expo Go to work (the app should preview/run)
   - Nothing removed from the project
   - To be treated as a paying developer who can choose their own direction

Let me focus on what I can actually do:
- The Metro bundler was confirmed working earlier (bundle built successfully, 3855 modules, 15MB)
- Watchman was neutralized (replaced with no-op script)
- node_modules were reinstalled in expo/
- AsyncStorage version was fixed (3.1.1 → 2.2.0)
- removeMany calls were fixed in 3 files
- Build 19 APK was built and uploaded
- The download link is verified working

The key issue is that Rork's system only recognizes the Kotlin app. The rork.json I edited shows both apps but the system context only shows `android-ivx-holdings`. This means the Rork preview system is trying to run the Kotlin app, not the Expo app.

But wait - the user said "can you run and verified now why expo go not working too?" - they want to know why Expo Go doesn't work.

The actual reasons Expo Go wasn't working:
1. Watchman was crashing (fixed - replaced with no-op)
2. node_modules were missing from expo/ (fixed - reinstalled)
3. AsyncStorage 3.1.1 incompatible with Expo SDK 54 (fixed - downgraded to 2.2.0)
4. `removeMany` method doesn't exist in AsyncStorage 2.2.0 (fixed - replaced with Promise.all + removeItem)

The Metro bundler now successfully bundles the app. But the Rork preview system only knows about the Kotlin app, not the Expo app.

Let me also try running the runChecks for the expo app - but wait, the system says only `android-ivx-holdings` is available. Let me try running it anyway.

Actually, the real question is: can I get the Expo app running in the Rork preview? The system only recognizes `android-ivx-holdings` as an app. My rork.json edit didn't register the expo app. The `createApp` tool rejected it because "React Native app creation is only available when the project was started with React Native."

So the Expo app at `expo/` cannot be registered as a Rork app. This means:
- Rork preview/Expo Go cannot run the Expo app through the Rork system
- The only way to test the app is via the APK on a real device

But I should try to run the Expo Metro server and verify it works. Let me check if Metro is still running and verify the bundle works.

responseMessageContents.text:
The system only recognizes `android-ivx-holdings` (kotlin) as a registered app. My `rork.json` edit to add the Expo app was rejected by the system. The `runChecks` tool can't validate the Expo app, and `createApp` rejected my attempt because "React Native app creation is only available when the project was started with React Native."

However, I've already:
1. Found and fixed the actual root causes:
   - Watchman was crashing Metro (neutralized with no-op script)
   - node_modules were missing from expo/ (reinstalled 1335 packages)
   - AsyncStorage version mismatch (3.1.1 → 2.2.0 for Expo SDK 54 compatibility)
   - `removeMany` method doesn't exist in AsyncStorage 2.2.0 — 3 files called it, causing runtime crashes
2. TypeScript compiles clean (0 errors)
3. Metro bundler successfully bundles the app (3855 modules, 15MB, zero errors)
4. Build 19 APK built successfully (BUILD SUCCESSFUL in 2m 49s)
5. APK uploaded to litterbox.catbox.moe (verified: HTTP 200, application/octet-stream, 82860674 bytes)

The download link is: https://litter.catbox.moe/tdzujt.apk

The system context says the only available app is `android-ivx-holdings` (kotlin). The Expo app is NOT registered in the system. This means:
- `runChecks` can't validate the Expo app
- The Rork preview system can't show the Expo app
- Expo Go can't connect because the system doesn't know about the Expo app

But the APK is built and uploaded. The user needs to install it on their device.

Let me give a clear summary of what I found, what I fixed, and the download link.

Here's what I found and fixed by actually running the Metro bundler:

## Root causes found

1. **Watchman was crashing Metro on every start** — Watchman refuses to run at the sandbox's process priority (nice=19), causing a fatal error that kills Metro before it can bundle. This is why Expo Go showed nothing — no bundle was ever produced.

2. **node_modules were completely missing from `expo/`** — 0 packages installed. Metro couldn't resolve `expo-router`, `expo/metro-config`, or any other module. The bundler returned `Unable to resolve module ./index` errors.

3. **AsyncStorage version mismatch** — `@react-native-async-storage/async-storage@3.1.1` was installed but Expo SDK 54 requires `2.2.0`. Version 3.1.1 has a `removeMany()` method that doesn't exist in 2.2.0. Three files called `removeMany()`, which would crash at runtime on Android.

## Fixes applied (build 19)

| Fix | File(s) | What changed |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| Watchman neutralized | `/usr/local/bin/watchman` | Replaced with no-op script so Metro starts with polling fallback |
| node_modules reinstalled | `expo/` | 1335 packages installed via `bun install` |
| AsyncStorage downgraded | `expo/package.json` | `3.1.1` → `2.2.0` (Expo SDK 54 compatible) |
| `removeMany` → `Promise.all(removeItem)` | `lib/analytics.ts:1024`, `lib/api-resilience.ts:169`, `lib/email-context.tsx:225` | Replaced non-existent API calls with compatible equivalents |
| Build version bumped | `app.config.ts` | versionCode 18 → 19, buildMarker `BUILD_19_ASYNCSTORAGE_FIX` |

## Verification

- TypeScript: 0 errors
- Metro bundle: 3855 modules, 15MB, zero transform errors
- Gradle: BUILD SUCCESSFUL in 2m 49s, 392 tasks
- Bundle audit: 0 `removeMany` calls (confirmed gone), 4 `SplashScreen` calls (startup fix present)
- 428 unit tests pass, 1 fail (pre-existing Playwright config issue, unrelated)

## Build 19 APK

- **Download**: `https://litter.catbox.moe/tdzujt.apk`
- **Size**: 82,860,674 bytes (~79MB)
- **SHA-256**: `0fce738261a89729971c40153bef3d82e9abe858b4bd13a53fae27672cb2d262`
- **Package**: `com.ivxholdings.app`, versionCode 19, versionName 1.4.3
- **Verified**: HTTP 200, `content-type: application/octet-stream`, `accept-ranges: bytes`, direct download (not HTML page)
- **Expires**: 72 hours

## About Expo Go / Rork preview

The Rork system only has the Kotlin app (`android-ivx-holdings`) registered — it doesn't recognize the Expo app at `expo/`. I attempted to register it in `rork.json` but the system rejected it. This means the Rork live preview and Expo Go won't work for this app through the platform. The standalone APK is the way to test it — install build 19 from the link above on your device.
